Merge pull request #1263 from iOfficeAI/zynx/fix/duplicate-user-message-on-navigation

fix(messages): skip duplicate user message when msg_id already exists

commit.message:
fix(messages): skip duplicate user message when msg_id already exists

When navigating from home page to conversation, a race condition could
cause user messages to appear doubled. The DB query and the backend
IPC user_content event both resolve close together; if DB loads first,
the subsequent addOrUpdateMessage call would append the content to the
existing message instead of deduplicating it.

Fix: in composeMessageWithIndex, skip the update when a text message
with position 'right' (user message) already exists in the list.
AI streaming messages (position 'left') continue to append as before.

commit.message:
fix(conversation): narrow acp session extra type to fix TS errors

acpSessionId and acpSessionUpdatedAt only exist on the acp variant's
extra type. Guard the field assignment with a type === 'acp' check so
TypeScript can narrow correctly, and cast the result as TChatConversation
to satisfy the discriminated union constraint.

Merge pull request #1259 from iOfficeAI/fix/builtin-assistant-readonly-regression

fix(settings): restore builtin assistant editing permissions

commit.message:
fix(settings): restore builtin assistant editing for Main Agent and Save button

The `isReadonlyAssistant` flag was combining `isBuiltin || isExtensionAssistant`,
which made builtin assistants fully read-only. Previously, builtin assistants
only locked identity fields (name, description, avatar, prompt) while allowing
Main Agent switching and saving.

Split the logic so that:
- Extension assistants remain fully read-only
- Builtin assistants only lock identity fields and prompt content
- Save button and Main Agent dropdown stay enabled for builtin assistants

Also added a permission matrix comment at the file header for future reference.

Closes #1258

commit.message:
fix: 修复新会话继承旧会话 ACP session 上下文的 bug

问题描述：
在同一个工作目录下，点击 tabs 栏的+号按钮或右上角+号按钮创建新会话时，
新会话会沿用了当前会话的 acpSessionId，导致 ACP 后端尝试恢复旧会话的
session，从而继承了完整的上下文，直接撑爆 Token 限制。

根本原因：
新会话创建时错误地复制了旧会话的 extra.acpSessionId 和
extra.acpSessionUpdatedAt 字段。

修复方法：
在 ChatConversation.tsx（右上角+号按钮）和 ConversationTabs.tsx（tabs
栏+号按钮）创建新会话时，显式清除 extra.acpSessionId 和
extra.acpSessionUpdatedAt 字段，确保新会话创建全新的 ACP session。

影响范围：
- ChatConversation.tsx: 第 82-93 行
- ConversationTabs.tsx: 第 144-150 行

fix(auto-update): use architecture-specific update channel (#1201)

Configure electron-updater to select the correct update manifest based on
platform and architecture, preventing ARM64 Windows users from downloading
the x64 installer.

Channel mapping:
- Windows ARM64 → latest-win-arm64.yml
- macOS ARM64 → latest-mac-arm64.yml
- macOS x64 → latest-mac-x64.yml
- Linux ARM64 → latest-linux-arm64.yml
- Windows/Linux x64 → latest.yml (default)

Merge pull request #1252 from iOfficeAI/fix/eager-worker-spawn-on-conversation-click

fix: prevent unnecessary Electron Helper process spawning on conversation click

commit.message:
fix: prevent unnecessary Electron Helper process spawning on conversation click

Read-only IPC providers (getMode, getModelInfo, getConfigOptions,
getSlashCommands) were using WorkerManage.getTaskByIdRollbackBuild()
which creates AgentManager instances (and forks utility processes)
when no cached task exists. Since these providers are called eagerly
on component mount (AcpModelSelector, AgentModeSelector, etc.),
every conversation click spawned a persistent Electron Helper process.

Switch these read-only providers to WorkerManage.getTaskById() which
only performs a cache lookup and returns default values when no task
exists. Worker processes are now only created on actual user actions
(sendMessage, setModel, setMode, etc.).

Also remove dead code: codexConversationBridge.ts (empty function,
Codex now uses ACP protocol).

Closes #1251

Merge pull request #1239 from cnJasonZ/feat/ppio-models

feat(models): add PPIO as preset LLM provider

commit.message:
fix: resolve memory leak, double render and dead code issues

This commit addresses all issues identified in the PR review:

1. Memory leak caused by duplicate useCronJobsMap() instantiation
   - Remove useCronJobsMap() from useConversationActions
   - Pass markAsRead as parameter instead
   - Reduces IPC listeners from 6 to 3

2. Double render on conversation switch
   - Extract setActiveConversation to update ref without state change
   - Use setActiveConversation in useEffect instead of markAsRead
   - Ensures activeConversationIdRef stays synced with route

3. Dead code and redundant dependencies
   - Remove unused trigger state variable
   - Remove unreadConversations from useMemo dependencies (redundant)

Flow:
- Click conversation → markAsRead → update ref + remove unread (1 render)
- Route change → setActiveConversation → update ref only (no render)

fix(cron): fix unread red dot indicator not disappearing on conversation click

Fixed the issue where the unread red dot indicator remained visible after clicking on a conversation with unread cron executions. The problem was caused by multiple components calling useCronJobsMap() independently, creating separate state instances.

Changes:
- Remove useCronJobsMap() call from ConversationRow component
- Pass getJobStatus as a prop from parent component (WorkspaceGroupedHistory)
- Add useEffect in WorkspaceGroupedHistory to call markAsRead when conversation id changes
- Add unreadConversations to useMemo dependencies to trigger re-renders
- Ensure single source of truth for unreadConversations state

The fix ensures that all components share the same unreadConversations state, so when markAsRead is called, the UI updates correctly and the red dot disappears.

feat(models): add PPIO as preset LLM provider

Add PPIO (https://api.ppinfra.com/v3/openai) as a preset provider
using OpenAI-compatible protocol.
chore(git): ignore local worktrees

refactor(i18n): replace hardcoded locale checks with i18n keys for mode/model labels

Remove isZhLocale, buildDefaultModelLabel, and formatModeDisplayLabel
from agentUiDisplay.ts — these bypassed the i18n system by hardcoding
Chinese translations in code.

- agentMode.permission, agentMode.smart, agentMode.bypassPermissions
- common.defaultModel

commit.message: chore: bump version to 1.8.24
docs(readme): remove outdated development section from all READMEs, move to docs/

fix(codex): resolve mode switch errors and Full Auto permission behavior

- Skip ACP session/set_mode for Codex backend to avoid "Invalid params"
  (codex-acp bridge uses MCP and does not support this ACP method)
- Sync yoloMode with currentMode so Full Auto auto-approves correctly
- Initialize yoloMode from sessionMode in constructor and legacy migration
- Add codex to yoloModeValues for legacy config migration

refactor: address PR review - remove unstable agents, add i18n, unify UI style

- Remove GeminiThinkingSelector and all Gemini thinking level IPC/worker code
- Remove Claude Code fallback (enableFallback, FALLBACK_THINKING_OPTIONS)
- Limit AcpConfigSelector to CONFIG_OPTION_SUPPORTED_BACKENDS (codex only)
- Add i18n translations for reasoning_effort in 6 locales
- Add Menu.ItemGroup title to AcpConfigSelector for UI consistency
- Replace enableFallback/compact props with backend prop

feat: add reasoning effort selector for Gemini, ACP, and Codex (#1211)

- Add GeminiThinkingSelector component with pill-button dropdown
- Add AcpConfigSelector component for dynamic ACP config options
- Implement GeminiAgent.setThinkingLevel() via registerRuntimeModelOverride
- Add IPC bridge channels for get/set thinking level and config options
- Integrate selectors into GeminiSendBox, AcpSendBox, and CodexSendBox
- Include fallback thinking options for Claude Code (best-effort)

commit.message:
refactor: address post-merge review findings from PR #1161

- fix(sandboxWorker): replace misleading security boundary comments with
  accurate description of Worker Thread isolation model; add TODO for
  future vm.runInNewContext enforcement

- fix(ExtensionRegistry): replace direct state mutation in initialize(),
  disableExtension(), and enableExtension() with immutable Map.set()
  pattern per project coding standards

- fix(AcpAgentManager): replace direct mutation of existing.message.content
  in queueBufferedStreamTextMessage with immutable spread update

- fix(lifecycle): remove unnecessary `as any` casts; use typed
  extension.manifest.lifecycle directly (field is defined in
  ExtensionManifestSchema)

- chore(vitest): expand coverage.include with all src files that have
  existing unit tests (agent/acp, common, renderer/utils, extensions);
  use glob patterns for extension resolvers; remove files without tests

- docs(CLAUDE.md): add tests/e2e/ to test structure; add test:e2e command
update UI

fix(test): skip i18n-packaged test when renderer output is missing

commit.message:
fix(test): skip i18n-packaged test when renderer output is missing

Previously the test only checked for app.asar existence, causing it to
run and throw 'Renderer output directory not found' when a stale app.asar
exists but out/renderer has been cleaned. Now both app.asar and
out/renderer must be present for the test to run.

commit.message: docs: rewrite CLAUDE.md and extract architecture details

fix(acp): implement Codex session resume via session/load

Codex ACP bridge ignores the generic resumeSessionId parameter in
session/new, causing every app restart to create a brand-new session
with no conversation history.

Add loadSession() method to AcpConnection that calls the ACP
session/load endpoint. Codex bridge's load_session() internally calls
resume_thread_from_rollout to restore full conversation history from
disk.

In AcpAgent.createOrResumeSession(), Codex backend now uses the
dedicated session/load method. Falls back to session/new if load fails.

Resume strategy per backend:
- Codex: dedicated ACP session/load method
- Claude/CodeBuddy: session/new with _meta.claudeCode.options.resume
- Others: session/new with generic resumeSessionId param
chore: remove accidentally created file
style: fix prettier formatting in base.css

refactor(acp): extract claude-agent-acp version into named constants

Define CLAUDE_ACP_BRIDGE_VERSION and CLAUDE_ACP_NPX_PACKAGE in acpTypes.ts,
mirroring the existing CODEX_ACP_* pattern, to avoid hardcoded package strings.

fix(acp): add two-phase retry to connectCodex and unify npx spawn logic

- Add Phase 1/Phase 2 --prefer-offline retry to connectCodex, matching
  the existing pattern in connectClaude and connectCodebuddy. Fixes
  Windows startup failure when @zed-industries/codex-acp is not cached
  locally (e.g. after upgrading from 1.8.20 to 1.8.21+).

- Extract spawnAndSetupNpxBackend to replace the three separate
  spawnAndSetupClaude / spawnAndSetupCodex / spawnAndSetupCodebuddy
  methods. Accepts an optional { extraArgs, detached } options bag so
  CodeBuddy's SIGTTOU workaround is handled without duplicating spawn
  boilerplate.
fix(ci): stabilize mac smoke version check and prevent startup hangs
fix(channels): auto-disable stale unavailable plugins on startup
test(e2e): fix channels coming-soon locator ambiguity
test: cover extension and no-extension e2e

feat: add extension e2e test specs

- ext-lifecycle: extension load/unload/enable/disable tests

- ext-permissions: permission grant/deny/prompt tests

- ext-ipc-queries: IPC query/response roundtrip tests

feat: add E2E navigation helpers and justfile recipe

- Add navigateToExtensionsPage helper for extension e2e tests

- Export new helpers from index.ts

- Add justfile recipe for e2e testing

fix: embed screenshots in E2E HTML report for better CI debugging

- Add manual screenshot-on-failure in custom Electron page fixture via testInfo.attach()

- Merge report/results/screenshots into single CI artifact

- Add E2E report upload step to pr-checks.yml for all platforms
feat: refine e2e flows and split PR artifact workflow
fix(settings): resolve white screen and extension i18n issues, preserve E2E tests
fix: align pr-checks workflow with upstream (no-frozen-lockfile, linux deps)
feat: improve extension webui and channel support
chore: sync local changes
fix: resolve pre-commit check failures for e2e-and-extensions branch
chore: fix lint and prettier issues for extensions and e2e
test(ci): make i18n static-import assertion quote-agnostic

fix(ci): dynamically detect unpacked dir for macOS x64 cross-compile

On arm64 runners, electron-builder produces 'mac-x64/' instead of
'mac/' when cross-compiling for x64. Add a detect-unpacked step that
probes all known candidate directories before uploading the artifact.

Also update the matrix unpacked_dir hint for macos-x64 to 'mac-x64'.

refactor(ci): split build-test into build + build-verify, E2E reuses build artifacts

- Split 'build-test' into 'build' (compile & package) and 'build-verify' (smoke tests)
- Build job uploads unpacked directories as artifacts (unpacked-{platform})
- Build job uploads installers as separate artifacts (installers-{platform})
- E2E tests now depend on build job and download pre-built unpacked artifacts
  instead of rebuilding from scratch — saves ~20min per platform
- E2E runs on 3 natively-runnable platforms: linux, macos-arm64, windows-x64
- build-verify downloads installer artifacts and runs smoke tests independently
- Downstream jobs (review, check-external) depend on both build + build-verify
- Restore POSIX exec permissions after artifact download on Linux/macOS
- Artifact retention: 1 day for build artifacts, 14 days for E2E reports

feat(e2e): run E2E tests against packaged app on all 3 platforms

- fixtures.ts: support packaged mode (E2E_PACKAGED=1) launching from
  out/*-unpacked/ directories; dev mode still available for local testing
- pr-checks.yml: E2E job now builds with --pack-only (vite + asar, no
  installer) then runs Playwright from the unpacked executable
- Three platforms: linux (ubuntu + xvfb), macOS-arm64, Windows-x64
- Fix 403 permission error: issues: read -> issues: write
- Fix Linux chrome-sandbox SUID error: --no-sandbox in CI
- Separate e2e-summary job for PR comment aggregation
- Also runs test:packaged:i18n to verify i18n assets in asar

perf(e2e): optimize E2E tests - 61% faster (3.6m → 1.4m)

- Replace fixed waitForTimeout with event-driven waits (waitForFunction,
  waitFor, MutationObserver) across all spec files
- Refactor navigateTo() to use requestAnimationFrame + content detection
  instead of hardcoded 2s sleep
- Split monolithic extensions.e2e.ts (485 lines) into 6 focused files
  for better parallel execution across workers
- Add screenshot gating via E2E_SCREENSHOTS env var (test.skip)
- Add E2E CI job to pr-checks.yml with:
  - Playwright HTML report uploaded as artifact
  - E2E screenshots uploaded as artifact
  - Automatic PR comment with test results and report download link
- Add waitForSettle() helper for consistent event-driven settling
- Improve goToChannelsTab() with text-based tab matching for reliability
fix: restore missing playwright.config.ts

feat: extension system core + e2e test infrastructure

- Add extension system: loader, registry, dependency resolver, hot reload
- Add 7 contribution resolvers: themes, skills, assistants, ACP adapters, channel plugins, MCP servers, WebUI
- Add extension examples: hello-world, e2e-full, acp-adapter, ext-feishu
- Add E2E test suite with Playwright: app-launch, navigation, channels, extensions, ACP agent, WebUI
- Integrate extensions into main process, IPC bridge, and renderer
- Add theme CSS sync from extensions, CssThemeSettings UI updates
- Update AcpDetector and AcpAgentManager for extension-provided adapters
- Update ChannelManager and PluginManager for extension channel plugins
- Add API routes for extension management

## What's Changed
* fix(cron): fix unread red dot indicator not disappearing on conversation click by @cdxiaodong in https://github.com/iOfficeAI/AionUi/pull/1216
* feat(models): add PPIO as preset LLM provider by @cnJasonZ in https://github.com/iOfficeAI/AionUi/pull/1239

* fix(auto-update): use architecture-specific update channel (#1201) by @gobylor in https://github.com/iOfficeAI/AionUi/pull/1254
* fix(settings): restore builtin assistant editing permissions by @kaizhou-lab in https://github.com/iOfficeAI/AionUi/pull/1259
* fix: 修复新会话继承旧会话 ACP session 上下文的 bug by @cdxiaodong in https://github.com/iOfficeAI/AionUi/pull/1253
* chore: bump version to 1.8.25 by @piorpua in https://github.com/iOfficeAI/AionUi/pull/1260
* fix(conversation): narrow acp session extra type to fix TS errors by @piorpua in https://github.com/iOfficeAI/AionUi/pull/1261
* chore: add type-check step to AGENTS.md and bump-version command by @piorpua in https://github.com/iOfficeAI/AionUi/pull/1262
* fix(messages): skip duplicate user message when msg_id already exists by @piorpua in https://github.com/iOfficeAI/AionUi/pull/1263

## New Contributors

* @gobylor made their first contribution in https://github.com/iOfficeAI/AionUi/pull/1254

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/iOfficeAI/AionUi/compare/v1.8.24...v1.8.25

releases.name: v1.8.24
releases.body: ## What's New ### Features - Reasoning effort – Choose thinking strength (e.g. low/medium/high) for Codex to balance speed and depth. - Unified chips and layout – Model and permission chips are unified, and the chat, preview, and workspace layout is cleaned up. - Slash command menu – The slash command menu has a clearer, more consistent look and is easier to scan. - Extension system – The app supports a pluggable extension system with an E2E test setup for add‑ons. ## What's Changed * 基本本地Windows打包做了一些修复 by @JAVA-LW in https://github.com/iOfficeAI/AionUi/pull/1058 * style(sendbox): refine slash command menu visual design by @ringringlin in https://github.com/iOfficeAI/AionUi/pull/1140 * fix: normalize electron-updater metadata to ensure deterministic release builds by @loonghao in https://github.com/iOfficeAI/AionUi/pull/1195 * chore: update bun.lock with testing library dependencies by @piorpua in https://github.com/iOfficeAI/AionUi/pull/1198 * fix(acp): two-phase retry for Codex/Claude ACP bridge with named version constants by @piorpua in https://github.com/iOfficeAI/AionUi/pull/1212 * docs: rewrite CLAUDE.md to be lean and accurate by @piorpua in https://github.com/iOfficeAI/AionUi/pull/1217 * feat(commands): add pr-review and bump-version commands by @piorpua in https://github.com/iOfficeAI/AionUi/pull/1219 * fix(test): skip i18n-packaged test when renderer output is missing by @piorpua in https://github.com/iOfficeAI/AionUi/pull/1220 * update wx-group qr code by @IceyLiu in https://github.com/iOfficeAI/AionUi/pull/1222 * fix(acp): implement Codex session resume via session/load by @audichuang in https://github.com/iOfficeAI/AionUi/pull/1215 * refactor(acp): extract parseSessionCapabilities to eliminate duplication by @piorpua in https://github.com/iOfficeAI/AionUi/pull/1224 * feat: extension system core + E2E test infrastructure by @loonghao in https://github.com/iOfficeAI/AionUi/pull/1161 * refactor: address post-merge review findings from PR #1161 by @piorpua in https://github.com/iOfficeAI/AionUi/pull/1227 * update UI by @IceyLiu in https://github.com/iOfficeAI/AionUi/pull/1226 * chore: migrate oss-commit to project-level command and rename to oss-pr by @piorpua in https://github.com/iOfficeAI/AionUi/pull/1229 * feat: add reasoning effort selector for Gemini, ACP, and Codex (#1211) by @audichuang in https://github.com/iOfficeAI/AionUi/pull/1213 * fix(codex): resolve mode switch errors and Full Auto permission behavior by @IceyLiu in https://github.com/iOfficeAI/AionUi/pull/1234 * docs(readme): remove outdated development section, move to docs/ by @kuishou68 in https://github.com/iOfficeAI/AionUi/pull/1235 * chore: bump version to 1.8.24 by @kaizhou-lab in https://github.com/iOfficeAI/AionUi/pull/1236 * feat(ui): unify model/permission chips and polish chat+preview+workspace layout by @ringringlin in https://github.com/iOfficeAI/AionUi/pull/1231 * style: remove extra blank line in AgentPillBar by @kaizhou-lab in https://github.com/iOfficeAI/AionUi/pull/1238 **Full Changelog**: https://github.com/iOfficeAI/AionUi/compare/v1.8.23...v1.8.24

readme_html:
📋 Quick Navigation

✨ Cowork in Action · 🤔 Why Choose AionUi? · 🚀 Quick Start · 💬 Community

Cowork — AI Agents That Work Alongside You

AionUi is more than a chat client. It's a Cowork platform where AI agents work alongside you on your computer — reading files, writing code, browsing the web, and automating tasks. You see everything the agent does, and you're always in control.

Traditional AI Chat Clients AionUi (Cowork)
AI can operate on your files Limited or No Yes — built-in agent with full file access
AI can execute multi-step tasks Limited Yes — autonomous with your approval
Remote access from phone Rarely WebUI + Telegram / Lark / DingTalk
Scheduled automation No Cron — 24/7 unattended
Multiple AI Agents at once No Claude Code, Codex, OpenClaw, Qwen Code, and 12+ more — auto-detected, unified interface
Price Free / Paid Free & Open Source

Built-in Agent — Install & Go, Zero Configuration

AionUi ships with a complete AI agent engine. Unlike tools that require you to install CLI agents separately, AionUi works the moment you install it.

  • No CLI tools to install — the agent engine is built in
  • No complex setup — sign in with Google or paste any API key
  • Full agent capabilities — file read/write, web search, image generation, MCP tools
  • Ready-to-use assistants — 11+ built-in professional assistants (Cowork, PPTX Generator, PDF to PPT, 3D Game, UI/UX Pro Max, and more) ready to use immediately

Multi-Agent Mode — Already Have CLI Agents? Bring Them In

If you already use Claude Code, Codex, or Qwen Code, AionUi auto-detects them and lets you Cowork with all of them — alongside the built-in agent.

Supported Agents: Built-in Agent (zero setup) • Claude Code • Codex • Qwen Code • Goose AI • OpenClaw • Augment Code • iFlow CLI • CodeBuddy • Kimi CLI • OpenCode • Factory Droid • GitHub Copilot • Qoder CLI • Mistral Vibe • Nanobot and more

  • Auto Detection — automatically recognizes installed CLI tools
  • Unified Interface — one Cowork platform for all your AI agents
  • Parallel Sessions — run multiple agents simultaneously with independent context
  • MCP Unified Management — configure MCP (Model Context Protocol) tools once, automatically sync to all agents — no need to configure each agent separately

Any API Key, Full Cowork Agent Power

Other AI apps give you a chatbox with your API key. AionUi gives you a full Cowork agent.

Your API Key What You Get
Gemini API Key (or Google login — free) Gemini-powered Cowork Agent
OpenAI API Key GPT-powered Cowork Agent
Anthropic API Key Claude-powered Cowork Agent
Ollama / LM Studio (local) Local model Cowork Agent
NewAPI Gateway Unified access to 20+ models

Same agent capabilities — file read/write, web search, image generation, tool use — regardless of which model powers it. AionUi supports 20+ AI platforms including cloud services and local deployments.

Comprehensive Platform Support:

  • Official Platforms — Gemini, Gemini (Vertex AI), Anthropic (Claude), OpenAI
  • Cloud Providers — AWS Bedrock, New API (unified AI model gateway)
  • Chinese Platforms — Dashscope (Qwen), Zhipu, Moonshot (Kimi), Qianfan (Baidu), Hunyuan (Tencent), Lingyi, ModelScope, InfiniAI, Ctyun, StepFun
  • International Platforms — DeepSeek, MiniMax, OpenRouter, SiliconFlow, xAI, Ark (Volcengine), Poe
  • Local Models — Ollama, LM Studio (via Custom platform with local API endpoint)

AionUi also supports NewAPI gateway service — a unified AI model hub that aggregates and distributes various LLMs. Flexibly switch between different models in the same interface to meet various task requirements.

Extensible Assistants & Skills

Extensible assistant system with 12 built-in professional assistants and custom skill support. Create and manage your own assistants and skills.

  • Create Custom Assistants — Define your own assistants with custom rules and capabilities
  • Manage Skills — Create, enable, and disable skills for any assistant to extend AI capabilities

AionUi includes 12 professional assistants with predefined capabilities, extendable through custom skills:

  • 🤝 Cowork — Autonomous task execution (file operations, document processing, workflow planning)
  • 📊 PPTX Generator — Generate PPTX presentations
  • 📄 PDF to PPT — Convert PDF to PPT
  • 🎮 3D Game — Single-file 3D game generation
  • 🎨 UI/UX Pro Max — Professional UI/UX design (57 styles, 95 color palettes)
  • 📋 Planning with Files — File-based planning for complex tasks (Manus-style persistent markdown planning)
  • 🧭 HUMAN 3.0 Coach — Personal development coach
  • 📣 Social Job Publisher — Job posting and publishing
  • 🦞 moltbook — Zero-deployment AI agent social networking
  • 📈 Beautiful Mermaid — Flowcharts, sequence diagrams, and more
  • 🔧 OpenClaw Setup — Setup and configuration assistant for OpenClaw integration
  • 📖 Story Roleplay — Immersive story roleplay with character cards and world info (SillyTavern compatible)

Custom Skills: Create skills in the skills/ directory, enable/disable skills for assistants to extend AI capabilities. Built-in skills include pptx, docx, pdf, xlsx, mermaid, and more.

💡 Each assistant is defined by a markdown file. Check the assistant/ directory for examples.

Cowork from Anywhere

Your 24/7 AI assistant — access AionUi from any device, anywhere.

  • WebUI Mode — access via browser from phone, tablet, or any computer. Supports LAN, cross-network, and server deployment. QR code or password login.

  • Chat Platform Integration

    • Telegram — Cowork with your AI agent directly from Telegram
    • Lark (Feishu) — Cowork through Feishu bots for enterprise collaboration
    • DingTalk — AI Card streaming with automatic fallback
    • Slack and more platforms coming soon

Setup: AionUi Settings → WebUI Settings → Channel, configure the Bot Token.

Remote Internet Access Tutorial

✨ Cowork in Action

Scheduled Tasks — Cowork on Autopilot

Set it up once, the AI agent runs automatically on schedule — truly 24/7 unattended operation.

  • Natural Language — tell the agent what to do, just like chatting
  • Flexible Scheduling — daily, weekly, monthly, or custom cron expressions
  • Use Cases: scheduled data aggregation, report generation, file organization, reminders
  • Conversation-Bound — Each scheduled task is bound to a conversation, maintaining context and history
  • Automatic Execution — Tasks run automatically at scheduled times, sending messages to the conversation
  • Easy Management — Create, modify, enable/disable, delete, and view scheduled tasks anytime

Real-World Examples:

  • Daily weather report generation
  • Weekly sales data aggregation
  • Monthly backup file organization
  • Custom reminder notifications

Preview Panel — Instantly View AI-Generated Results

10+ formats: PDF, Word, Excel, PPT, code, Markdown, images, HTML, Diff — view everything without switching apps.

  • Instant Preview — after the agent generates files, view results immediately without switching apps
  • Real-time Tracking + Editable — automatically tracks file changes; supports live editing of Markdown, code, HTML
  • Multi-Tab Support — open multiple files simultaneously, each in its own tab
  • Version History — view and restore historical versions of files (Git-based)

Supported Preview Formats:

  • Documents — PDF, Word (.doc, .docx, .odt), Excel (.xls, .xlsx, .ods, .csv), PowerPoint (.ppt, .pptx, .odp)
  • Code — JavaScript, TypeScript, Python, Java, Go, Rust, C/C++, CSS, JSON, XML, YAML, Shell scripts, and 30+ programming languages
  • Markup — Markdown (.md, .markdown), HTML (.html, .htm)
  • Images — PNG, JPG, JPEG, GIF, SVG, WebP, BMP, ICO, TIFF, AVIF
  • Other — Diff files (.diff, .patch)

Smart File Management — Automated File Operations

Batch renaming, automatic organization, smart classification, file merging — the Cowork agent handles it for you.

  • Auto Organize — Intelligently identify content and auto-classify, keeping folders tidy
  • Efficient Batch — One-click rename, merge files, say goodbye to tedious manual tasks
  • Automated Execution — AI agents can independently execute file operations, read/write files, and complete tasks automatically

Use Cases:

  • Organize messy download folders by file type
  • Batch rename photos with meaningful names
  • Merge multiple documents into one
  • Auto-classify files by content

Excel Data Processing — AI-Powered Analysis

Deeply analyze Excel data, automatically beautify reports, and generate insights — all powered by AI agents.

  • Smart Analysis — AI analyzes data patterns and generates insights
  • Auto Formatting — Automatically beautify Excel reports with professional styling
  • Data Transformation — Convert, merge, and restructure data with natural language commands
  • Report Generation — Create comprehensive reports from raw data

Use Cases:

  • Analyze sales data and generate monthly reports
  • Clean and format messy Excel files
  • Merge multiple spreadsheets intelligently
  • Create data visualizations and charts

AI Image Generation & Editing

Intelligent image generation, editing, and recognition, powered by Gemini

  • Text-to-Image — Generate images from natural language descriptions
  • Image Editing — Modify and enhance existing images
  • Image Recognition — Analyze and describe image content
  • Batch Processing — Generate multiple images at once

Image generation model configuration guide

Document Generation — PPT, Word, Markdown

Automatically generate professional documents — presentations, reports, and more — with AI agents.

  • PPTX Generator — Create professional presentations from outlines or topics
  • Word Documents — Generate formatted Word documents with proper structure
  • Markdown Files — Create and format Markdown documents for documentation
  • PDF Conversion — Convert between various document formats

Use Cases:

  • Generate quarterly business presentations
  • Create technical documentation
  • Convert PDF to editable formats
  • Auto-format research papers

Personalized Interface Customization

Customize with your own CSS code, make your interface match your preferences

  • ✅ Fully Customizable — Freely customize interface colors, styles, layout through CSS code, create your exclusive experience

Multi-Task Parallel Processing

Open multiple conversations, tasks don't get mixed up, independent memory, double efficiency

  • ✅ Independent Context — Each conversation maintains its own context and history
  • ✅ Parallel Execution — Run multiple tasks simultaneously without interference
  • ✅ Smart Management — Easy switching between conversations with visual indicators

🤔 Why Choose AionUi Over Claude Cowork?

AionUi is a free and open-source Multi-AI Agent Desktop. Compared to Claude Cowork which only runs on macOS and is locked to Claude, AionUi is its full-model, cross-platform enhanced version.

Dimension Claude Cowork AionUi
OS macOS Only macOS / Windows / Linux
Model Support Claude Only Gemini, Claude, DeepSeek, OpenAI, Ollama, ...
Interaction Desktop GUI Desktop GUI + WebUI + Telegram / Lark / DingTalk
Automation Manual only Cron scheduled tasks — 24/7 unattended
Cost $100/month Free & Open Source

Deep AI Office Scenario Support:

  • File Management: Intelligently organize local folders and batch rename with one click.
  • Data Processing: Deeply analyze and automatically beautify Excel reports.
  • Document Generation: Automatically write and format PPT, Word, and Markdown documents.
  • Instant Preview: Built-in 10+ format preview panels, AI collaboration results instantly visible.

🚀 Quick Start

System Requirements

  • macOS: 10.15 or higher
  • Windows: Windows 10 or higher
  • Linux: Ubuntu 18.04+ / Debian 10+ / Fedora 32+
  • Memory: 4GB+ recommended
  • Storage: 500MB+ available space

Install

# macOS via Homebrew
brew install aionui

Get Started in 3 Steps

  1. Install AionUi
  2. Sign in with Google account or enter any API key
  3. Start Coworking — the built-in AI agent is ready to go
